title: abutilon picardae
intro: Abutilon picardae, also known as the desert holly or red-leaved abutilon, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Abutilon picardae include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. Abutilon picardae can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or salves for its medicinal uses, and is often used in traditional folk medicine.
